query: effect of UC-63 expression on treatment sensitivity in prostate cancer

retrieved_docs.title: Prostate Cancer -- Medical Oncology -- Areas of Future Research
retrieved_docs.content: Second-generation antiandrogens fail, at least in part, due to androgen receptor mutations or splicing adjustments. These actions result in cell reactivation. Various mechanisms of reducing androgen receptor protein levels and activity are being investigated, including androgen receptor nuclear localization inhibition, N-terminal suppression, heat-shock protein blockage, and proteasome-mediated accelerated degradation. [385] Researchers at the University of California, Davis, have developed a molecule called LX-1, targeting both AR and the AKR1C3 enzyme, intending to circumvent the development of castration resistance. Androgen receptor proteolysis targeting chimeras are also a promising avenue, with the ability to induce degradation of the AR target rather than just inhibition, and is currently being investigated in clinical trials. [386]

retrieved_docs.title: Nuclear Medicine Applications in Prostate Cancer -- Technique or Treatment -- Treatment Protocols for Lu-177-PSMA Therapy
retrieved_docs.content: Treatment response may be assessed using the tenets of the prostate cancer workgroup (PCWG3) but should reasonably include PSA response, follow-up imaging at PSMA PET, and diagnostic CT. In addition, patient symptom response and tolerance of therapy should also be key factors in assessing treatment. Quantitative pain assessments and quality of life should be performed before, during, and after therapy.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 3 -- Community 2566
retrieved_docs.content: Prazosin is classified as an antiadrenergic alpha receptor agent, meaning it functions as an antagonist of alpha‑adrenergic receptors. By blocking these receptors, prazosin induces vasodilation, lowers peripheral vascular resistance, and reduces blood pressure, making it useful in the management of hypertension. Additionally, its alpha‑blocking properties help relax smooth muscle in the prostate and bladder neck, providing therapeutic benefit for patients with benign prostatic hyperplasia. Understanding that prazosin belongs to the broader class of antiadrenergic alpha receptor agents informs clinicians about its mechanism of action, potential drug interactions, and side‑effect profile, thereby guiding appropriate patient selection and monitoring.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 3 -- Community 1462
retrieved_docs.content: Testosterone propionate is contraindicated in patients with hyperplasia of the prostate, because its androgenic activity can exacerbate prostatic enlargement and worsen the condition.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 29 -- Level 3 -- Community 1808
retrieved_docs.content: Genitourinary symptoms and ill‑defined conditions arise within the urinary system and frequently co‑occur with prostatic disorders, seminal calculi, and both localized and metastatic prostate cancer; they are also clinically associated with prostate cancer. The prostate is an organ that belongs to the urinary system and serves as the anatomic site for prostate‑related diseases, including metastatic prostate cancer. Prostatic disorders are situated in the prostate, and other male genital disorders are likewise located in the prostate, representing subcategories of prostatic disorders. These other male genital disorders are linked to screening activities. Together, these relationships emphasize that disturbances of the prostate and associated genitourinary symptoms are central to the presentation, co‑occurrence, and clinical management of prostate disease, particularly cancer, and underscore the importance of appropriate diagnostic screening.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 3 -- Community 2582
retrieved_docs.content: Testosterone‑5‑alpha reductase inhibitors for benign prostatic hypertrophy constitute a drug class that includes finasteride and dutasteride. Finasteride may be used to treat prostate tumor (ورم البروستاتا), reflecting its therapeutic role in prostate‑related disease. Dutasteride, as another member of this inhibitor class, is similarly employed in the management of benign prostatic hypertrophy, highlighting the broader application of 5‑alpha reductase inhibition in treating conditions of the prostate.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2446
retrieved_docs.content: Finasteride, a 5‑α‑reductase inhibitor, may be used to treat prostate tumor (ورم البروستاتا) by reducing the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone, which can help shrink benign prostatic hyperplasia and may also have therapeutic effects on prostatic neoplasms. Its potential benefit in managing prostate tumor highlights the importance of hormonal modulation in urologic oncology and informs clinicians when considering pharmacologic options for patients with prostatic growth abnormalities.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 2 -- Community 1087
retrieved_docs.content: The prostate (prostata) is a key anatomical site within the male genital system and is the focus of specific diagnostic procedures that are applied when the prostate tissue has been partially excised. This organ serves as the anatomic location for metastatic prostate cancer (سرطان البروستاتا النَّقيلي), linking the disease directly to the prostate tissue. In addition, the prostate is identified as the location of a broader category of prostatic disorder (اضطراب بروستاتي), which functions as a parent classification for other male genital disorders. These other male genital disorders are themselves situated in the prostate and are closely associated with screening activities (التحصّي). Together, these relationships highlight the central role of the prostate in both disease manifestation and diagnostic evaluation, emphasizing the importance of targeted procedures and screening in the management of prostate‑related conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 3 -- Community 2262
retrieved_docs.content: Genitourinary symptoms and ill‑defined conditions frequently co‑occur with حصاة حالبية (ureteral stone), سرطـان البروستاتا النَّقيلي (metastatic prostate cancer), and فرط تنسج بروستاتي حميد (benign prostatic hyperplasia). This pattern of co‑occurrence indicates that patients who present with nonspecific genitourinary complaints may also have underlying stone disease, advanced prostate malignancy, or benign prostatic enlargement, underscoring the need for thorough diagnostic assessment to identify and manage these potentially related conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 3 -- Community 2263
retrieved_docs.content: Transurethral resection of the prostate (TURP) is a surgical procedure that is clinically associated with both تضخم بروستاتي (benign prostatic hyperplasia) and سرطان البروستاتا (prostate cancer). TURP is frequently performed to alleviate urinary obstruction caused by prostate enlargement, and it can also be utilized in the management of prostate cancer, for example to obtain tissue for diagnosis or to provide palliative relief of obstructive symptoms.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2530
retrieved_docs.content: Other male genital disorders are considered a subcategory of prostate disorder (اضطراب بروستاتي) and are linked to the practice of screening (التحصّي). Recognizing that other male genital disorders fall under the broader category of prostate disorder and are associated with screening emphasizes the importance of appropriate diagnostic evaluation in the management of these conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 1 -- Community 227
retrieved_docs.content: palliation. Additional male genital disorders located in the prostate are categorized under prostate disorder and are connected to screening or prophylaxis (التحصّي). This integrated framework illustrates how bladder disorders, prostate disorders, urinary stones, and prostate malignancies interrelate, and how a spectrum of diagnostic and therapeutic procedures—particularly those directed at the prostate—are organized to manage both benign and malignant conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 4 -- Community 2802
retrieved_docs.content: The prostate (prostata) is the specific anatomic site associated with metastatic prostate cancer (سرطان البروستاتا النَّقيلي), indicating that this malignant disease originates in the prostate gland and has the potential to spread beyond its primary location, a relationship that is essential for accurate diagnosis, staging, and therapeutic planning.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 3 -- Community 1913
retrieved_docs.content: The CLINICAL CLASSIFICATION OF NEOPLASMS OF THE GENITOURINARY SYSTEM provides a hierarchical framework in which cancer of prostate is positioned as a subcategory. Within the cancer of prostate category, the specific malignant prostate tumor grouping identified as أورام البروستاتا الخبيثة (SMQ) is included as a member. This structured classification helps organize prostate neoplasms for accurate diagnosis, research, and treatment planning.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 3 -- Community 1886
retrieved_docs.content: Genitourinary symptoms and ill-defined conditions are noted to co‑occur with حصاة حالبية and also with سرطان البروستاتا النَّقيلي, indicating that patients who present with vague or nonspecific genitourinary complaints may simultaneously have ureteral stones (حصاة حالبية) or prostate cancer (سرطان البروستاتا النَّقيلي). Recognizing this association can prompt clinicians to consider targeted diagnostic evaluations for these specific urologic pathologies when faced with ambiguous genitourinary presentations, thereby improving detection and management of underlying stone disease or malignancy.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 31 -- Level 3 -- Community 1517
retrieved_docs.content: Transrectal biopsy of prostate and open prostatectomy are both classified under the broader category of عمليات البروستاتا (prostate procedures). As prostate procedures, they represent distinct interventions used in the evaluation and management of prostate disease: the transrectal biopsy of prostate is a diagnostic technique that obtains tissue samples for histologic assessment, while open prostatectomy is a surgical approach that removes prostate tissue to treat conditions such as benign prostatic hyperplasia or localized prostate cancer. Recognizing that both procedures belong to the same overarching group of prostate operations helps clinicians select appropriate diagnostic or therapeutic strategies within the context of prostate health care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 3 -- Community 1723
retrieved_docs.content: Transurethral resection of prostate (TURP) is clinically associated with فرط تنسج بروستاتي حميد (benign prostatic hyperplasia), تضخم بروستاتي (prostatic enlargement), and سرطان البروستاتا (prostate cancer). TURP is commonly performed to relieve urinary obstruction caused by benign prostatic hyperplasia and other forms of prostatic enlargement, providing symptomatic improvement and facilitating bladder emptying. In the context of prostate cancer, TURP may be used to obtain tissue for histopathological diagnosis or to palliate obstructive symptoms in advanced disease. By addressing both benign and malignant prostatic conditions, TURP plays a pivotal role in the therapeutic and diagnostic management of patients with prostate-related disorders.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 3 -- Community 1523
retrieved_docs.content: Operations by approach encompasses open prostatectomy, indicating that open prostatectomy is a specific surgical approach classified under Operations by approach. Both open prostatectomy and Transrectal biopsy of prostate are linked to عمليات البروستاتا (prostate operations), showing they belong to the broader category of prostate surgeries. Transrectal biopsy of prostate is also categorized as a diagnostic procedure for the male genital system, reflecting its role in evaluating prostate pathology. Clinically, عمليات البروستاتا are associated with خلل وظيفي في الانتصاب (erectile dysfunction), highlighting a potential adverse effect of prostate surgeries on sexual function. Understanding these relationships aids clinicians in selecting appropriate surgical approaches, recognizing diagnostic options, and counseling patients about possible functional outcomes.
